channel.title: Alex Leung's Blog
channel.description: Notes on software systems, AI-assisted coding, deep learning, Next.js static sites, and small browser experiments.

item.title: Farming Expensive Coding Agent Sessions
item.description: My coding-agent sessions are more expensive now. The useful spend is the part that turns mistakes into better tests, graders, prompts, and checks.

item.title: Camping Indoors in San Francisco
item.description: My first week in San Francisco: paperwork, an unfurnished apartment, two cats, Bay Trail runs, and waiting for furniture.

item.title: Coding Agents for Inspectable Browser Tools
item.description: Building frontend-only tools for load flow, Mandelbrot zooming, optimizer behavior, and event-loop ordering gave me concrete ways to inspect older technical models.

item.title: Dropout as Shared-Parameter Bagging
item.description: A concrete way to understand dropout in deep learning: sampled subnetworks with shared weights, standing in for a much more expensive ensemble.

item.title: Studying with the 11-Inch M3 iPad Air
item.description: Notes on the 11-inch iPad Air M3 as a lightweight reading, annotation, and remote-workflow setup for studying.

item.title: One Manifest for Responsive Images
item.description: I replaced scattered responsive-image conventions in a Next.js static site with one generated manifest for variants, dimensions, and runtime lookup.

item.title: Make Internal Tools Cheap to Try
item.description: A conversation with colleagues about lower-friction AI-assisted prototypes for small internal tools.

item.title: Codex for Site Edits, Sora for Visual Sketches
item.description: How Codex made small website edits easier to make from a phone, while Sora still feels more useful for exploratory visuals than finished work.

item.title: Representation, Efficiency, and Learnability
item.description: Goodfellow Chapter 6 sharpened how I separate representation, parameter efficiency, and optimization when thinking about deep feedforward networks.

item.title: Math Foundations for Deep Learning
item.description: Part I of Goodfellow, Bengio, and Courville works best as shared vocabulary for later chapters, with proof details still worth supplementing.

item.title: Planning and Verification in AI-Assisted Coding
item.description: How my AI-assisted coding workflow shifted from quick snippets to clearer briefs, review checkpoints, and build/lint/test verification.

item.title: Static Markdown Inside This Next.js Site
item.description: How I added a repo-backed Markdown blog to my static Next.js portfolio without adding a CMS or runtime admin surface.
